source.title: Temu pursues 'local fulfillment' to dodge tariffs - Supply Chain Dive

extractions.claim: Temu operates 13 self-owned warehouses globally (10 Europe, 2 US, 1 Mexico). US warehouses handle 15-20% of US volume in 2025, projected 20-25% by 2026.
extractions.evidence: Temu is building physical infrastructure, operating 13 self-owned warehouses globally (10 in Europe, 2 in the US, 1 in Mexico). By 2025, Temu's self-operated warehouses in the US handle 15-20% of total US goods volume, projected to increase to 20-25% by 2026.

verification.verifier_notes: Warehouse count from TechBuzzChina Temu Watch. Percentage projections are analyst estimates.

extractions.claim: Temu US monthly users dropped from 34M (May 2024) to under 8M (May 2025) after de minimis elimination. Sales fell 17% YoY in first week after tariff hikes.
extractions.evidence: Temu's user base in the U.S. plummeted from nearly 34 million monthly users in May 2024 to fewer than 8 million in May 2025. Sales at Temu fell 17% year-on-year in the week following tariff hikes (Bloomberg Second Measure).

extractions.claim: Warehouse model tax advantage: direct mail taxed at retail value, warehouse inventory taxed at wholesale. Semi-managed model fees: $0.12/cubic ft/day storage + $0.85/order fulfillment.
extractions.evidence: An insider points out that direct mail is taxed at retail, while inventory sent to overseas warehouses is taxed at wholesale. This is a key cost advantage. Semi-managed model: storage rate $0.12 per cubic foot per day, order fulfillment ~$0.85 per order.

verification.verifier_notes: TechBuzzChina and Alibaba SmartBuy merchant guide

extractions.claim: Temu growth targets: 40-60% growth in 2025, 30% growth in 2026. Global GMV reached $35B in H1 2025, with 60% growth from non-US markets.
extractions.evidence: Growth target for 2025 set between 40% and 60%, and 30% growth expected in 2026. Global GMV reached $35B in H1 2025, with 60% growth from non-U.S. markets like Europe and Southeast Asia.

verification.verifier_notes: Analyst estimates; PDD does not officially disclose Temu GMV targets

extractions.claim: Price increases of 10-17% still maintain overall price advantage vs competitors. Temu adapted by shifting to 60% price cuts and stabilized US sales after tariffs dropped to 54% in July 2025.
extractions.evidence: Temu's low-price strategy has been affected to a certain extent, but even if the price increases by 10% to 17%, its overall price advantage still exists. Temu's shift to semi-managed logistics and 60% price cuts stabilized U.S. sales after tariffs dropped to 54% in July 2025.

verification.verifier_notes: Multiple analyst reports. Price advantage claim needs verification against current competitor pricing.
